justification: All three conflicting sources (PM, Finance, Operations) are explicitly named and the logical contradiction is clearly articulated. A formal escalation is produced with the contradiction type, affected stakeholders, and resolution options. No acceptance criteria are produced (the array is empty). The proposed options are clearly labeled as options requiring stakeholder resolution, not presented as fact. The response clearly states what is needed to proceed (stakeholder alignment). Confidence is appropriately low at 40.

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: contradiction explicitly identified with all three sources named (PM's cancel-before-shipment, Ops' invoice-at-placement, Finance's no-cancel-after-invoice). Formal escalation produced with type 'contradiction' before any acceptance criteria. acceptance_criteria array is empty — no criteria produced. Reconciliation options are presented clearly as options requiring stakeholder sign-off, not as invented facts. Response clearly states what is needed to proceed (stakeholder meeting to resolve which rule yields). Confidence level is 45, well below the 70 fail threshold.

observations.text: Empty acceptance_criteria array — correctly refused to produce criteria for an unresolvable spec
All three conflicting sources explicitly named and traced to their stakeholder origins (PM, Finance, Operations)
Escalation section clearly articulates the logical chain: placed → invoiced (BR-3), invoiced → no cancel (BR-2), therefore no cancel ever — contradicting BR-1
Options A-D are presented as possibilities requiring stakeholder decision, not as invented reconciliations presented as fact
Confidence at 45 with reasoning that directly references the contradiction
rejection_reasons section reinforces that implementation cannot proceed as-is
Edge cases all marked UNDEFINED, reinforcing that no behavior can be specified under contradictory rules
The recommendation for Option D/B leans slightly toward prescribing a solution rather than purely escalating, though it is clearly framed as requiring stakeholder alignment

trials.justification: Bird explicitly identifies the contradiction and names all three sources. However, it fails the 'no acceptance criteria produced' criterion in spirit by producing detailed reconciliation options (A-D) with a specific recommendation (Option B), which constitutes an invented reconciliation. While flagged as needing stakeholder agreement, the recommendation is presented with enough conviction to guide a decision rather than purely escalating. Additionally, the empty acceptance_criteria array is correct, but the options/recommendation section effectively does the work that acceptance criteria would do — pre-resolving the contradiction rather than purely escalating it.

observations.text: All three conflicting sources (PM, Finance, Operations) explicitly named with their respective rules
Contradiction clearly articulated as a logical impossibility, not just a disagreement
Acceptance criteria array is empty — no Given/When/Then produced for the cancellation feature
Confidence level at 45, well below 70 threshold
Formal escalation produced with type 'contradiction' and rejection_reasons section
Edge cases correctly marked as UNDEFINED rather than resolved

Provides four detailed reconciliation options with a specific recommendation (Option B), going beyond pure escalation into solution design — this is an invented reconciliation even though it's flagged as needing agreement
The recommendation for Option B is presented with enough detail and conviction that a team could act on it without the required stakeholder resolution meeting
